question: The chart shows time allocation on a typical day. Which of the following statements is correct according to the actual percentage values?

options.A: Sleep accounts for a larger share of time than Work.
options.B: Work and Sleep together account for more than two-thirds of the day.
options.C: Chores take up more time than Sleep.
options.D: Leisure takes up more time than Work.

explanation: This pie chart uses dual encoding: slice angle represents percentage, and the radius also varies with percentage, so slice area differences are exaggerated. That can make the largest category look overly dominant. Work: 35%; Sleep: 32%; Chores: 18%; Commute: 8%; Leisure: 7%.
